diagrams.description: Behavioral Economics integrates psychological insights into economic theory to explain real human decision-making. This flowchart illustrates the process: starting from the ultimate goal of understanding and improving real-world decisions, it highlights the role of psychological drivers including cognitive biases (availability, anchoring) and framing effects, which are formalized in key theories like Prospect Theory, Bounded Rationality, and Mental Accounting. These drivers lead to suboptimal outcomes such as under-saving and panic. The chart then shows how Nudge Theory (choice architecture) intervenes to mitigate these biases, ultimately achieving better individual and societal welfare.
